factors.data_availability: high — large well-known longitudinal cohorts exist (ADNI, ROSMAP, NACC, NCRAD); AminoChain sources add commercial options
factors.org_discoverability: high — ADNI/ROSMAP are highly cited; C-BIG is indexed; ProteoGenex has web presence
factors.biomarker_specificity: low — MMSE/MoCA are standard clinical scales, not rare biomarkers

expected_failure_modes: Pipeline finds ADNI/ROSMAP/NACC easily but these are academic cohorts with formal DUA access — lead times are 3-12 months, not commercial procurement
Commercial biobanks (ProteoGenex, BIOMEDICA CRO) listed as having longitudinal specimens when longitudinal tracking is not their primary model — verification required
MMSE/MoCA scores assumed available because cohort is 'dementia research' — cognitive score linkage requires clinical data infrastructure, not just specimen banking
Dementia subtypes conflated — AD, vascular, Lewy body, FTD have different biology; pipeline may not separate these without explicit source evidence
DNA availability at commercial sources inferred from serum/plasma availability — separate extraction step not always performed
Timepoint interval assumed (e.g., annual) without explicit documentation from source
